title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Program Brief: Unicode Version Evidence
summary: Visually identical words count separately, compatibility characters collapse without disclosure, lowercasing misses caseless matches, and the report cannot link normalized tokens back to original text. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the unicode version evidence increment in versioned reversible Unicode poli…
objectives: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.
Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence — Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_unicode_normalization_4(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-cwmg0
# Competency: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence.
# Case 4801: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_unicode_version_evidence_cwmg0(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_cwmg0 = "cwmg0-0gmwc-4801"
    assert evidence_variant_cwmg0
    evidence_axes_cwmg0 = (
        "corpuscwmg0",
        "policy0gmwc",
        "revision4801",
        "commandcwmg04801",
        "report0gmwc4801",
        "test4801cwmg0",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_cwmg0)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Unicode Version Evidence model · Theory Unicode Version Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Unicode behavior is timeless and cannot alter a report between Python releases.
